statement: David H. Bailey is Senior Scientist (retired) at the Computational Research Department, Lawrence Berkeley National Laboratory.
David H. Bailey explicitly names Jonathan M. Borwein, Peter Borwein, Simon Plouffe, and Richard Crandall as long-time collaborators on his self-maintained homepage.

Bailey, Borwein and Crandall co-authored 'On the Khinchin constant and continued fractions', Mathematics of Computation 66 (1997), pp 1619-1632 [the foundational reference for the Khinchin-signature manuscript content].

Bailey and Broadhurst co-authored 'Parallel integer relation detection: techniques and applications', Mathematics of Computation 70 (2001), no. 236, 1719-1736 [cited as manuscript reference 3 in source-baustms.tex].

The Experimental Mathematics journal underwent an editorial board reboot led by Al Kasprzyk (current Editor-in-Chief); Pieter Belmans joined as a new board member, announced in his 20 Feb 2024 blog post.

The Taylor & Francis editorial board page for Experimental Mathematics (uexm20) returned HTTP 403 on direct fetch on 2026-05-18; Wayback Machine has no cached snapshot reachable by direct URL pattern.
note: Filter A for all 3 named reviewers is therefore UNVERIFIED-PROBABLE-PASS via secondary search.

statement: David J. Broadhurst is affiliated with The Open University, UK, and presented 'Resurgent integer sequences' at the Rutgers Experimental Mathematics Seminar on 6 Feb 2025.

David J. Broadhurst's recent (2024-2025) publications include a book chapter in 'Lie Theory and Its Applications in Physics' (Springer Proceedings vol 473, 2025) and 'Richard Feynman's talent for finding things out' in J. Phys.: Conf. Ser. 2877 (2024).
Brigitte Vallée is Directrice de Recherche CNRS at Laboratoire GREYC, Université de Caen Normandie, France.

Brigitte Vallée co-authored 'Analysis of generalized continued fraction algorithms over polynomials' (with Berthé, Nakada, Natsui), Finite Fields and Their Applications 73 (2021) 101849; and a 2025-forthcoming paper 'Shannon Weights for binary dynamical recurrent sources of zero entropy'.
Brigitte Vallée's principal long-term coauthor was Philippe Flajolet, who is NOT in the named BBC cluster {Bailey, Borwein, Crandall, Ferguson, Plouffe}.

Wadim Zudilin co-authored 'Three-step and four-step random walk integrals' with Jonathan M. Borwein, Armin Straub, and James Wan in Experimental Mathematics 22(1) 2013, pp 1-14; and 'Densities of short uniform random walks' with the same group in Canadian Journal of Mathematics 2012.
note: This direct Borwein coauthorship is the basis for excluding Zudilin from replacement candidates.

statement: Yann Bugeaud is an active researcher in Diophantine approximation and continued fractions at IRMA, Université de Strasbourg, with multiple 2024-2025 publications on continued-fraction expansions (Hurwitz CF metrical properties, Thue-Morse CF expansions).
There is no evidence that Yann Bugeaud directly coauthored any papers with David H. Bailey, Jonathan Borwein, or Richard Crandall.

Tanguy Rivoal is Directeur de recherche CNRS at the Institut Fourier, Université Grenoble Alpes; recent 2024 paper with Stéphane Fischler 'Transcendence of values of logarithms of E-functions' (arXiv:2409.18537).
There is no evidence of any coauthored paper between Tanguy Rivoal and any of David H. Bailey, Peter Borwein, Richard Crandall, Simon Plouffe, or Helaman Ferguson.

Tanguy Rivoal has co-authored papers with Wadim Zudilin (e.g., on irrationality of infinitely many odd zeta values), which makes him a 2nd-degree connection to Jonathan Borwein via the Zudilin-Borwein direct coauthorship.
Igor E. Shparlinski is Professor at the School of Mathematics and Statistics, UNSW Sydney, Australia, with an extensive (~2024-2026) publication output in computational and analytic number theory.

Igor E. Shparlinski co-edited 'Number Theory and Related Fields: In Memory of Alf van der Poorten' (Springer Proceedings in Mathematics & Statistics 43, 2013) with Jonathan M. Borwein and Wadim Zudilin.

Stéphane Fischler is a mathematician at Institut de Mathématique d'Orsay (Université Paris-Saclay) specializing in number theory, irrationality measures, and transcendence of values of E-functions; recent collaborator of Tanguy Rivoal (arXiv:2409.18537, 2024).
There is no indication that Stéphane Fischler coauthored any paper with Borwein or Bailey.

Papanokechi's public artefact trail consists exclusively of Zenodo deposits 10.5281/zenodo.19562751 (AEAL), 10.5281/zenodo.20173677 (Khinchin-signature concept), and 10.5281/zenodo.20173678 (Khinchin-signature v1.0). No coauthorship, acknowledgment, or correspondence with any of the named reviewers or replacement candidates is publicly findable.
